root_cause.text: The `prev_pkt` pointer in `MatroskaDemuxContext` was not correctly nulled out when the packet queue was cleared across all code paths, leaving a dangling pointer that could be accessed after its associated memory was freed.
root_cause.evidence: The patch moves `matroska->prev_pkt = NULL;` from `matroska_read_seek` directly into `matroska_clear_queue`, ensuring the pointer is properly cleared whenever the packet queue is emptied.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `prev_pkt` pointer in `MatroskaDemuxContext` was not correctly nulled out when the packet queue was cleared across all code paths, leaving a dangling pointer that could be accessed after its associated memory was freed.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch moves `matroska->prev_pkt = NULL;` from `matroska_read_seek` directly into `matroska_clear_queue`, ensuring the pointer is properly cleared whenever the packet queue is emptied.

intent_record.intent_summary: Reset the prev_pkt pointer to NULL when clearing the packet queue to prevent dangling pointers.
intent_record.what: Assigned NULL to the prev_pkt member of the matroska context struct.
intent_record.how: Prevents potential use-after-free or dangling pointer issues by ensuring the previous packet reference is cleared when the queue is emptied.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix incorrect clearing of the previous packet pointer during matroska read seeking.
intent_record.what: Removed the assignment of NULL to matroska->prev_pkt inside the timestamp search loop.
intent_record.how: Prevents the previous packet state from being incorrectly cleared during cluster parsing, maintaining correct internal packet state across loop iterations.

grouping_result.intent: Fix incorrect clearing of the previous packet pointer during matroska read seeking.

consistency_review.core_intent: Fix the clearing of the previous packet pointer to prevent dangling pointers.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the vulnerability by resetting the prev_pkt pointer to NULL when the packet queue is cleared, preventing the dangling pointer described in the root cause.

candidates.rationale: This function is responsible for clearing the packet queue but failed to null out the prev_pkt pointer.
This is where the prev_pkt pointer should have been set to NULL across all paths of the queue clearing.
This statement was in an incorrect scope (inside the loop) rather than being centralized inside matroska_clear_queue, leaving other code paths vulnerable.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement was placed incorrectly in specific code paths (like seek) instead of being centralized in the queue clearing function, leading to dangling pointers in other paths.
This is the location where the prev_pkt pointer should have been set to NULL across all paths when clearing the queue.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
matroska: Clear prev_pkt between seeks.

The new incremental parser doesn't always clear prev_pkt,
however the packet qu
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 7521c4bab28ff3a622171be5b39a6b210f4263f0
Author: Dale Curtis <dalecurtis@chromium.org>
Date:   Mon Apr 23 16:15:31 2012 +0000

    matroska: Clear prev_pkt between seeks.
    
    The new incremental parser doesn't always clear prev_pkt,
    however the packet queue is cleared when seeking. Which leads
    to a use-after-free.
    
    Verified using Valgrind.
    
    Signed-off-by: Dale Curtis <dalecurtis@chromium.org>
    Signed-off-by: Justin Ruggles <justin.ruggles@gmail.com>

diff -
bic_trace_step.reason: The diff clearly shows the addition of 'matroska->prev_pkt = NULL;' immediately before 'matroska_clear_queue(matroska);'. This fixes the dangling pointer vulnerability by correctly nulling out 'prev_pkt' when the packet queue is cleared during seeks.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
matroskadec: reorder some functions in a more logical order

Originally committed as revision 14604 to svn://svn.ffmpeg.
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 737c40da20de07fd1b1ca99699f896c84b03f0ff
Author: Aurelien Jacobs <aurel@gnuage.org>
Date:   Tue Aug 5 00:42:39 2008 +0000

    matroskadec: reorder some functions in a more logical order
    
    Originally committed as revision 14604 to svn://svn.ffmpeg.org/ffmpeg/trunk

diff --git a/libavformat/matroskadec.c b/libavformat/matroskadec.c
index 13508b08c9..4cdeb527e0 100644
--- a/libavformat/matroskadec.c
+++ b/libavformat/matroskadec.c
@@ -594,6 +594,24 @@ static int ebml_read_ascii(ByteI
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `matroska_clear_queue` frees all packets and resets `matroska->packets` and `matroska->num_packets`, but it completely omits clearing the `prev_pkt` pointer. This leaves a dangling pointer if `prev_pkt` was pointing to one of the freed packets.
